title: Create Registry in Docker forTravis CI
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Creates and runs a registry using Docker inside of Travis CI that the Buildah Authenitcation tests can use.  There's a bunch of extraneous stuff doing some Docker stuff that I'd like to keep in for now until I can get the certificates in play.  Currently the authentication test turn off tls verification.  Will be changing that in a follow up submit.  Once that's all settled, I'll do something similar in kpod so we can test kpod login and other functions there.

comments.body: Looks like Travis failed on `sudo apt-get -qq update`. What does it fail on when it comes to registries @TomSweeneyRedHat?
Try dropping the `-qq` so that we'll get an error message when it fails.  I don't really have strong feelings about suppressing the output from `apt-get`; it's just what I'd seen in examples.
@TomSweeneyRedHat  I am having the same problem in https://github.com/kubernetes-incubator/cri-o/pull/1015
Yeah!  Docker registry seems to be up in Travis.  Let's see if I can run authentication tests against it.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #305) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:boom: Invalid `.papr.yml`: failed to parse 1st testsuite: at least one of 'build' or 'tests' required.

:boom: Invalid `.papr.yml`: failed to parse 1st testsuite: Schema validation failed:
 - Key 'install' was not defined. Path: ''.
 - Key 'script' was not defined. Path: ''..
@TomSweeneyRedHat I think this PR needs some work.

@rhatdan, I've been poking at it.  It's a pain as you tweak it then have to wait for a github cycle to see the results.  Finally got Travis to talk to the Docker Registry, think I'm getting close to registry too.  One of my PTO projects.

Green lights!  Docker registry is running in Travis.  Would like to get this in if possible.  There's some extraneous Docker commands and other info like stuff that I'm doing, I'd like to keep those in until the follow up PR for certificates goes in.
Well Darn it, thought I had.  Hope it sticks this time.
@TomSweeneyRedHat You have to change the commit message on the PR as well.  It says WIP and DONOTMERGE.

@rhatdan I did a "git commit --amend -s" this time and changed the verbiage inside the editor and saved that then did a fetch/rebase/push.  Holler if I need to tweak it otherwise, I think it's changed up now.

:pushpin: Commit c4289fa has been approved by `rhatdan`

:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
